composite_description: BEAR cycle with DISLOCATION -- highest-conviction setup, derivatives crowded short but no fundamental break
brief:
BTC is deep in a macro downtrend—price below 200d MA, 9.4% lower over five days, and Fear & Greed at 12 signals capitulation. The setup qualifies as BEAR/CRYPTO_DISLOCATION: derivatives data unavailable, but the absence of fresh funding extremes alongside a -3.9% overnight decline and sustained technical breakdown argues this is forced liquidation and retail panic rather than structural repositioning. Altcoins (ETH, SOL) tracking BTC weakness without relative outperformance confirms risk-off, not selective deleveraging.

The highest-conviction signal is the **extreme fear reading (12/100)** paired with **absence of crowded derivative shorts**—if shorts were truly packed, we would see violent recovery on any bounce. Instead, capitulation feels genuine and unhedged. ETH and SOL's underperformance (z-scores near -1) suggests institutional rotation is modest; the break is broad and indiscriminate. Dislocation thesis holds only if fundamentals (adoption, flow) remain intact through this drawdown.
